model.quant_mix: 216 Q4_K + 37 Q6_K + 145 F32 norms (drives both q4k_gemv and q6k_gemv)

comparator: llama.cpp acd79d603 (Windows CPU-only build) /completion, greedy (temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0), -ngl 0 -ctk f32 -ctv f32 -fa off --no-repack -c 8704
camelid_backend: q4_k_m runtime, RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB: ALL five buckets fit under the 15888-position VRAM-sized resident KV cap (build_resident_cuda_engine: free 5122 MiB - weights 2375 MiB - 512 headroom -> fits 15888 pos), so decode is fully GPU-resident (CUDA q4k_gemv/q6k_gemv) end-to-end for every bucket. No CPU-fallback.
execution_note: Single-model runs only: the llama.cpp oracle was captured alone and its server killed before camelid was started (engines never co-resident), to stay within host RAM.

buckets.note: NOT identical — benign greedy near-tie divergence (see near_tie detail); not claimed
token+text identical in this sweep, but held unpromoted to keep the promoted ladder a contiguous 512/1024 (the 2048 bucket between it and here is a near-tie divergence)
token+text identical in this sweep, but held unpromoted to keep the promoted ladder a contiguous 512/1024 (the 2048 bucket between it and here is a near-tie divergence)

near_tie.description: At generation index 14 (after the shared 14-token prefix) camelid's top token is ' llama' (leading to correct fact recall) and the llama.cpp oracle's is ' the' (leading the oracle into a degenerate 'the code is CMLD-2048' repetition loop). In camelid's own distribution the two candidates are separated by only 0.082 nat — a benign sub-0.1-nat near-tie well under the ~0.33-nat threshold used elsewhere in this repo. This is a lower-precision-quant greedy coin-flip, not a camelid decode error; camelid's continuation is the more sensible one. Because it is not token-identical to the oracle, the 2048 bucket is NOT promoted.

claim_boundary: PROMOTED: bounded 512/1024-context (446/968 Qwen3 tokens) raw-decode greedy parity for the exact Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M row ONLY — camelid GPU-resident CUDA decode (q4k_gemv/q6k_gemv) is token-AND-text-identical to the pinned llama.cpp acd79d603 oracle at 50 generated tokens on both buckets. DISCLOSED BUT NOT PROMOTED: the 4096/8192 buckets (4088/8253 tokens) were ALSO token-AND-text-identical in this same sweep, but the promoted ladder is held to a contiguous 512/1024 because the intervening 2048 bucket diverges at a documented benign sub-0.1-nat greedy near-tie (see near_tie; the oracle degenerates into a repetition loop, camelid recalls correctly). NOT claimed: the 2048 bucket, the 4096/8192 buckets pending a clean 2048, model-native/larger context, other Qwen3 sizes/quants, other K-quant files, thinking-mode, or throughput.
